prompts.scenario_background:
Using the reference frame/crop, recreate a clean gameplay background plate for the playable ad.

Asset: Environment Background
Description: A 2D cartoon landscape with rolling green hills, a cloudy sky, barbed wire fences, small trees, and a dirt ground with a red underground layer. Soft gradients and no thick outlines.
Gameplay role: Static backdrop for the gameplay.

=== STYLE LOCK (must match exactly across every asset in this game) ===
Overall: Casual 2D cartoon mobile game with thick black outlines, flat colors, and a sticker-like aesthetic.
Rendering technique: 2D vector-style cartoon
Line work: Thick, consistent black outlines on all foreground elements.
Palette: Muted earth tones for the environment, with saturated primary colors for characters, projectiles, and UI elements.
Shading: Flat colors with minimal shading on characters/props; soft gradients used sparingly in the background.
Scale / proportions: Chibi/stylized proportions, sticker-like characters and props.
DO NOT use any of these styles: pixel art, 3D rendered, photorealistic, voxel, painterly, highly detailed.
Match this style regardless of what the asset depicts — do not default to genre tropes (e.g. ninjas as pixel art, robots as voxel, fantasy as painterly) unless the lock above says so.

Preserve the original scene composition, camera angle, palette, and lighting.
Remove foreground characters, projectiles, UI, tutorial hands, and transient VFX when they cover the background.
Reconstruct hidden background details plausibly and keep the result seamless enough to sit behind gameplay.
Do not create a poster, logo, new character, or new UI.
Return an opaque rectangular background plate, not a transparent sprite.

notes: Opaque plate output; no alpha removal.
assets.name: Environment Background

prompts.scenario:
Using the reference crop, recreate ONLY this asset as a clean 2D mobile game sprite.

Asset: Player Castle Exterior
Category: castle
Description: A tall, grey stone castle with two blue-roofed towers, a wooden base, and tank treads. Thick black outlines and flat colors.
Gameplay role: The player's main base that houses units and takes damage.

=== STYLE LOCK (must match exactly across every asset in this game) ===
Overall: Casual 2D cartoon mobile game with thick black outlines, flat colors, and a sticker-like aesthetic.
Rendering technique: 2D vector-style cartoon
Line work: Thick, consistent black outlines on all foreground elements.
Palette: Muted earth tones for the environment, with saturated primary colors for characters, projectiles, and UI elements.
Shading: Flat colors with minimal shading on characters/props; soft gradients used sparingly in the background.
Scale / proportions: Chibi/stylized proportions, sticker-like characters and props.
DO NOT use any of these styles: pixel art, 3D rendered, photorealistic, voxel, painterly, highly detailed.
Match this style regardless of what the asset depicts — do not default to genre tropes (e.g. ninjas as pixel art, robots as voxel, fantasy as painterly) unless the lock above says so.

Preserve the source asset's silhouette, color family, proportions, camera angle, and readable details.
Improve video compression artifacts and make the asset crisp and production-quality.
Center the asset with comfortable transparent padding.

Remove everything that is not the asset: no background, no wall, no character, no hand cursor, no UI, no smoke/trail/impact particles unless explicitly part of the target.
Use a transparent background PNG when supported. If transparency is not preserved, use a plain simple background that can be removed cleanly.
Do not add new objects or change the gameplay read.

notes: Video crop is reference evidence; Scenario recreation supplies final pixels.

Asset: Green Goblin Unit
Description: A small green goblin cartoon character with horns and a red headband, holding a green cannon. Thick black outlines and flat colors.
Gameplay role: A player unit that shoots poison.

=== STYLE LOCK (must match exactly across every asset in this game) ===
Overall: Casual 2D cartoon mobile game with thick black outlines, flat colors, and a sticker-like aesthetic.

prompts.parts_sheet: Create a transparent 4x2 character parts sheet in the fixed slot order: head, body, arm_front, arm_back, leg_front, leg_back, weapon, shadow. No labels, no grid lines, no scenery.
prompts.segmentation_fallback: Use broad visual category prompts or bounding boxes only; SAM outputs masks, not painted color parts.

notes: Parts plus rig.json are the runtime target; generated parts sheet is the default, PSB is optional artist handoff only.

prompts.gemini_codegen:
Analyze this gameplay VFX asset and recreate it as procedural browser-game code, not as a Scenario-generated sprite.

Asset: Fire Explosion Impact
Description: A large, fiery explosion with bright yellow and orange colors and dark smoke.
Gameplay role: 

Return a compact implementation plan for a Phaser/Canvas playable ad:
- visual read at mobile scale,
- duration in milliseconds,
- color palette,
- particle counts,
- emitter positions,
- velocity ranges,
- size/alpha/rotation curves,
- blend mode,
- cleanup timing,
- TypeScript code for a reusable function.

The output should be deterministic enough to recreate the effect without needing a PNG sprite sheet.
Do not include external assets.

notes: No Scenario image generation by default; recreate with code and tune in engine.
assets.name: Fire Explosion Impact

prompts.scenario:
Using the reference crop, recreate ONLY the projectile body as a clean 2D mobile game sprite ready for direct engine use.

Asset: Green Poison Blob
Description: A small, bright green blob or flask.
Gameplay role: Projectile fired by the goblin unit.

=== STYLE LOCK (must match exactly across every asset in this game) ===
Overall: Casual 2D cartoon mobile game with thick black outlines, flat colors, and a sticker-like aesthetic.
Rendering technique: 2D vector-style cartoon
Line work: Thick, consistent black outlines on all foreground elements.
Palette: Muted earth tones for the environment, with saturated primary colors for characters, projectiles, and UI elements.
Shading: Flat colors with minimal shading on characters/props; soft gradients used sparingly in the background.
Scale / proportions: Chibi/stylized proportions, sticker-like characters and props.
DO NOT use any of these styles: pixel art, 3D rendered, photorealistic, voxel, painterly, highly detailed.
Match this style regardless of what the asset depicts — do not default to genre tropes (e.g. ninjas as pixel art, robots as voxel, fantasy as painterly) unless the lock above says so.

Reproduce the projectile's shape, silhouette, and color exactly.
Center it on a transparent background with comfortable padding.

STRICT EXCLUSIONS — do NOT include any of the following:
- smoke trails, exhaust plumes, fire trails, or motion streaks
- launch effects, impact particles, or debris
- glow auras, light flares, or bloom
- any visual effect that is NOT the hard body of the projectile

The game engine will add all visual effects (trails, explosions, glows) at runtime.
This sprite is the clean projectile object only — imagine it sitting still on a shelf with no motion.

notes: Video crop is reference evidence; Scenario recreation supplies final pixels.
assets.name: Green Poison Blob

Description: A realistic-looking human hand with a pointing index finger.
Gameplay role: Guides the player on where to tap and drag.

=== STYLE LOCK (must match exactly across every asset in this game) ===
Overall: Casual 2D cartoon mobile game with thick black outlines, flat colors, and a sticker-like aesthetic.
Rendering technique: 2D vector-style cartoon
Line work: Thick, consistent black outlines on all foreground elements.
Palette: Muted earth tones for the environment, with saturated primary colors for characters, projectiles, and UI elements.
Shading: Flat colors with minimal shading on characters/props; soft gradients used sparingly in the background.
Scale / proportions: Chibi/stylized proportions, sticker-like characters and props.
DO NOT use any of these styles: pixel art, 3D rendered, photorealistic, voxel, painterly, highly detailed.
Match this style regardless of what the asset depicts — do not default to genre tropes (e.g. ninjas as pixel art, robots as voxel, fantasy as painterly) unless the lock above says so.

Preserve the source asset's silhouette, color family, proportions, camera angle, and readable details.
Improve video compression artifacts and make the asset crisp and production-quality.
Center the asset with comfortable transparent padding.

Remove everything that is not the asset: no background, no wall, no character, no hand cursor, no UI, no smoke/trail/impact particles unless explicitly part of the target.
Use a transparent background PNG when supported. If transparency is not preserved, use a plain simple background that can be removed cleanly.
Do not add new objects or change the gameplay read.

notes: Prefer HTML/CSS for simple counters/labels; use Scenario UI LoRAs for graphical UI art.
assets.name: Tutorial Pointing Hand

prompts.gemini_codegen:
Analyze this gameplay VFX asset and recreate it as procedural browser-game code, not as a Scenario-generated sprite.

Asset: Fireball Smoke Trail
Description: A trail of brownish-orange puffy smoke clouds.
Gameplay role: 

Return a compact implementation plan for a Phaser/Canvas playable ad:
- visual read at mobile scale,
- duration in milliseconds,
- color palette,
- particle counts,
- emitter positions,
- velocity ranges,
- size/alpha/rotation curves,
- blend mode,
- cleanup timing,
- TypeScript code for a reusable function.

The output should be deterministic enough to recreate the effect without needing a PNG sprite sheet.
Do not include external assets.

notes: No Scenario image generation by default; recreate with code and tune in engine.
assets.name: Fireball Smoke Trail
